mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS shows a sustained increase, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals a tight labor market, which historically correlates with rising wage pressures and inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0%, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal a tightening monetary cycle, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a monetary easing cycle reduces borrowing costs for corporations, stimulating investment and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO rises, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A spike in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which increases bond yields and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer demand for residential construction, which correlates with broader economic optimism and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST rises, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) signals economic weakness, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 contracts, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
